ab: maqtu cak macingav su’u?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: maqtu, na malau.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: maqtu’ cak macingav su’u?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: ni, opa ni nak madia a qancaipan.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: lumaq asu maca tinaungquvali’ haip? asa cak tantungu su’uan maqtu?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: u na malau, na ha’an cak lumaq matala’ su’u.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: haza su al’u’an laqbing? asa cak macingav pailcicia itu Bunun tu takiqabasan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於布農族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the [Bunun] people.

ab: uka’ cak laqbing, na munba’av cak.
ch: B:明天我不在，我要去山上。
en: B: Tomorrow I’m not here, I’m going to the mountains.

ab: tan’a cak tu su’u dau ka malmananu maqanciap itu Bunun tu iniqumican. asa cak pascingav su’u.
ch: A:我聽說您對Bunun族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Bunun, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: ni amin nak madia a qancaipun, na langatlangat antalam su’u ina.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: maqtu cak siza’ i qaningu? na ni tu panpungulan.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?才不會忘記。
en: A: May I record? I won’t forget.

ab: u, na malau.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: pin su qamisan?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: masiva’un qan vau’in nak qa qamisan.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: ai vakai’ang asu ki.
en: A: You’re still young.

ab: ni! madaingazin cak.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: maisna’icaq qa asu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: maisnaha’an cak Tamazu’an.
en: B: I am from Damar Luan (Deli Village).

ab: Bunun amin na binano’az su?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是布農族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also [Bunun]?

ab: ni, Qalavang naip.
en: B: No, she’s a [Thayas].

ab: pia su qalinga qancaipun?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: qanciap cak malasBunun cin malasQalavang.
ch: B: 我會說布農語和泰雅語。
en: B: I can speak Bunun and Atayal.

ab: manaskal maqtu asu latuza’ pacingavan, macingav su’u paicicia qanup tu samu?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: na malau, qanglac cak munba’av qanup tu vakai’ang qabas.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: miqumicang, mailmananu’ su haip pa sintaqu’.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: mamantuk nak ka sinbazbaz, itu madadaingaz tu sintataqu.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: maqtu’in pan’iti haip pa ispabazbaz, tuza’ tu miqumicang sala su’u.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: ni tu makua, na muqnang patataqu maca lakuin dau.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: na mungausin cak mudan, na lakuin ata muqnang tan’a su’u tataqu.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: u! daukdauk.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
